raw.objective: “simulation was used to assess the impact of irregular dosing on topiramate (TPM) concentrations in noninduced (monotherapy/neutral cotherapy) and induced (adjunctive therapy with enzyme-inducing AEDs) states using a population pharmacokinetic (PK) model developed to predict steady-state plasma concentration–time profiles produced by once-daily Trokendi XR® and BID TPM-IR”
raw.covariates: Tested: - Included: EIAED (enzyme inducers) and WT on CL/F
raw.observations: Based on the simulations performed, dosing irregularities with once-daily Trokendi XR should pose no greater risk than with BID TPM-IR. In the event of a delayed or omitted Trokendi XR dose, TPM concentrations can be restored in noninduced and induced states by administering the delayed/omitted dose at any time during the next dosing interval or by adding the missed dose to the next scheduled dose
raw.dose: Multiple oral doses in healthy subjects
raw.structure: 2-compartment model with first-order absorption and elimination
raw.equations: CL/F = (theta1*(AED-1) + theta15*AED)*((WT/70)**theta14)*exp(eta1) Vc/F = theta2*exp(eta2) Q = theta3*exp(eta3) Vp/F = theta4*exp(eta4) if (TRT = IR & DOSE = morning) Ka = theta5*exp(eta5) Tlag = theta6 F1 = theta7 if (TRT = IR & DOSE = evening) Ka = theta8*exp(eta6) Tlag = theta9 F1 = theta10 if (TRT = XR) Ka = theta11*exp(eta7) Tlag = theta12 F1 = theta13*exp(eta8) Notes: Code for covariate effects on CL/F was derived based on description provided in the parameter table (supplemental material) No information were provided on the error model used for the analysis
raw.parameter_values: Theta1 = 1.30 Theta2 = 42 Theta3 = 1.70 Theta4 = 528 Theta5 = 2.25 Theta6 = 0.16 Theta7 = 1 Theta8 = 0.396 Theta9 = 0.16 Theta10 = 1.25 Theta11= 0.0920 Theta12 = 0.14 Theta13 = 1.11 Theta14 = 0.577 Theta15 = 1.75 Omega1 = 0.039 Omega2 = 0.01 Omega3 = 0.833 Omega4 = 1.402 Omega5 = 0.931 Omega6 = 0.437 Omega7 = 0.526 Omega8 = 0.052

study.dose: Multiple oral doses in healthy subjects
study.objective: “simulation was used to assess the impact of irregular dosing on topiramate (TPM) concentrations in noninduced (monotherapy/neutral cotherapy) and induced (adjunctive therapy with enzyme-inducing AEDs) states using a population pharmacokinetic (PK) model developed to predict steady-state plasma concentration–time profiles produced by once-daily Trokendi XR® and BID TPM-IR”

covariates.included: EIAED (enzyme inducers) and WT on CL/F
covariates.raw: Tested: - Included: EIAED (enzyme inducers) and WT on CL/F

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
